docs.title: 🐾 Welcome
docs.text: This private site contains all the up-to-date instructions for looking after Giles and Parker.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
1. Pick Parker up only when necessary, supporting both his chest and rear. Do not lift him by the chest alone.

  2. Giles can be quite unpredictable. Always watch your step and check before sitting down. I do not recommend picking him up. Better to guide him with voices and gestures while giving him space. Hold him securely and support both his chest and rear if you absolutely need to pick him up.

  3. Giles is hard of hearing. Always show him your hands first before touching him and announce your presence by tapping gently in front of him if you need to wake him up. Always touch him from under the chin and chest, never over his head, neck and back.

  4. Keep the bedroom door closed at all times.

  5. A foldable mattress with a fitted sheet is set up by the couch. Feel free to use it or sleep in the bedroom, whichever you prefer.

  6. To turn on/off lights in the living room or bedroom: "Alexa, living room/bedroom on/off," or "Alexa, lights on/off"

  7. Guide Parker to use the jumping cushion or ramp when getting on or off the couch.

  8. Parker does not use the ramp outside.

  9. Please do not walk the dogs. Indoor and yard playtime is sufficient.

  10. Please remove your shoes at the door. Disposable indoor slippers are provided.

  11. Please do not bring any guests.

  12. The dogs like to snuggle and sleep on blankets. Please keep the couch blankets spread out.

  13. You don’t need to pick up poop. However, if you do, please discard the waste bags in the Pet Genie bin by the porch.

  14. On rare occasions, the dogs may have poop hanging due to hair or soft stool, causing them to crouch-walk. Please use the provided gloves, poop bags, and wet wipes to help clean them if needed.

docs.title: Morning/Wake-up (7 am - 8 am)
docs.text:
• Potty

    • Check if the gate is closed and locked.
    • Watch out for people and animals in the fence or passing by since Parker likes to bark at them. Might want to let them pass before letting Parker out unless he really needs to go potty.
    • I let them be outside to sniff and explore as long as they like unless they are barking.
    • Feel free to stay inside by the door, but watch them at all times.
    • They come to the door when they want to come inside. Sometimes, Giles might get scared and linger by the door even when he needs to go. Make sure to give him enough time and space and he should go down the ramp to potty.
      • Giles tend to poop in the morning usually after breakfast, but sometimes right after waking up.
      • Both are good at signaling that they need to go outside, so you can rely on their cues and also heavily on the regular potty intervals.
        • Potty signals
          • Parker barks near the door or guides you to the door or sits/lies down by the door.
          • Giles crying or spinning for no apparent triggering reason (anything that could scare him or stress him out) or getting down from the couch and spinning/crying/
    • If you need them inside, you can say "let's go inside" or "come inside," and keep the door wide open. They usually come in then, but not always. Parker especially loves being outside.
    • If they have a paw up and/or not moving, they might have a pine needle stuck in their paw. Carefully check the paws and gently pull the needle out.

  • Parker's water bowl check

    • Parker always drinks water right after eating so his water bowl needs to be checked before feeding.
    • Rinse with hot tap water and fill with RO filtered water
      • If water bowl is exceptionally dirty, use dog dish sponge (pink smiley sponge wand) and dawn dish soap to wash it.

  • Hunger strikes from separation

    • Please keep me posted about any hunger strikes.
    • Keep offering water
    • Try giving a spoonful of cooked ground turkey (in the fridge) alone in their bowl

  • Breakfast

    • Right after morning potty
    • Empty their pre-portioned food in each of their bowls
      • Food packed in ziploc bags marked for Giles and Parker in fridge and freezer (move the bags in freezer to fridge when you get low on the food in the fridge bags)
    • Put two bowls together in microwave
    • Cover with small plates
    • Microwave for 50 seconds
    • Pour 1/4 shot glass of RO filtered water into Giles' bowl and 1/2 shot glass into Parker's
    • Mix their food with their own corresponding spoons
      • Separate spoons offered in case the bowls are not washed every time since Giles' would have medicine in it
    • (Breakfast only) Shake Giles' med bottle. Sprinkle 0.5 ml of his medicine into Giles' bowl. No mixing.
    • Rinse spoons right away for use later
    • Put Giles' and Parker's bowls on the mats on the floor
      • Generally, Giles' bowl goes on the mat closer to the kitchen but you might need to switch the bowls around depending on which mat Giles feels comfortable eating on
      • Parker barking at bowl
        • Means: 1) bowl is wobbly or sliding around when he's trying to eat, or 2) food is too hot
      • Helping with Giles' hesitation to eat
        • You sitting by Giles and his bowl
        • Or settling down away from him
        • Or moving the mat and bowl closer to him or rotating the bowl
        • Or letting Parker out when he finishes his food; Giles might start eating or join Parker outside, come right back in and start eating while you watch Parker by the door
        • Or hand-feeding (only as a last resort)

  • Potty right after eating

  • Parker's post-food play

    • Parker likes to lick/chew/play with his toy after eating and drinking water
    • He will most likely want to bring his toy and lie down by you while he does that. Or he might want you to engage with him. You can wiggle the toy or do a light tug of war. Keep toy and play on his ground level. No standing/jumping for toys or fetching.

  • Giles' water drinking

    • He usually requests water immediately after waking up and after eating.
    • He will most likely scratch the area around the water bottle to signal his need to drink water right after eating or whenever he wants water. Or he might lick his lips and look at the water bottle and you. Or he might whine and circle on the couch.
    • Put down large, folded pink towl to not soak the couch
    • Unscrew the clear button inside the bowl counter-clockwise and squeeze bottle and hold the squeeze to keep the water in the bowl
    • Tip the bottle sideway on top of towels and remain still. Giles will come and lick the side of the bowl to drink water. Expect lots of splashes!
    • As he drinks water, you can squeeze the bottle more to keep the water closer to the brim of the tipped bowl. He struggles to drink if the water level is lower.
    • He walks away once he's done drinking. If he just pauses from drinking without walking away, you should continue holding the water. He may resume drinking water.

docs.title: Daytime
docs.text:
• Potty every 3 - 5 hours or when they ask for it

    • Same instructions as above
    • Potty signals
      • Parker barks near the door or guides you to the door or sits/lies down by the door
      • Giles crying or spinning for no apparent triggering reason (anything that could scare him or stress him out) or getting down the ramp and spinning near the door

  • If Giles hasn't drunk water out of his bottle since waking up, feel free to offer it to him

  • Play requested by Parker

  • Quality chilling/snuggling/sleeping time for the dogs

    • Giles doesn't play with toys but he will be happy to come chill near you if he feels comfortable being around you.
    • They both love gentle pets.
      • Giles hates getting touched, especially without him knowing, or in a gripping motion, on the top of the head and back of the neck possibly due to developing neck arthritis. Always pet him gently directly under his chin and on the chest.

  • Mandatory snacking sessions at 11- 12 PM and 3 - 4 PM

    • To reduce bile build-up especially for Giles. Otherwise, he throws up and refuses food.
    • Duck jerky is broken into small pieces and stored in a ziploc bag on the kitchen countertop.
    • 3 pieces for Giles and 5 pieces for Parker per snacking session
    • They, especially Parker, love to play "Find It" with duck jerky pieces. You can hide them at various spots on the floor (behind toys, around the ramp corner, etc.) in the house. With Giles, I recommend you just give the treats directly to him so that you don't have to manage eager Parker lunging toward Giles. Also, we want to make sure Giles gets these treats and doesn't have an empty stomach for too long.
    • Giles always asks for treats by scratching his surroundings (sofa, cushions, blankets, and/or floor) around 11 - 12 PM and 3 - 4 PM. Even without his cues, please offer the snacks at the suggested times above and see if he takes them.

docs.title: Evening (5 pm - 7 pm)
docs.text:
• Parker's water bowl check

  • Dinner

    • Same "Breakfast" instructions except Giles does NOT get his medicine this time.
    • If they have been eating reliably, start adding the supplement for dinner only.
      • Put 1 flat scoop (1/2 tsp) of supplement (white power in ziploc) into Giles' bowl and 2 flat scoops into Parker's

  • Potty post-food

  • Parker's post-food play

  • Water for Giles if he requests it

  • Quality chillraxing time

  • Potty every 3 - 5 hours or when they ask for it

  • Maybe a snack session if Giles requests it

docs.title: Bedtime (10 pm - 11 pm)
docs.text:
• Duck jerky snack (3 - 5 pieces per dog) right before bed to prevent bile build-up
  • Final potty before bed
    • Watch out for animals, especially skunks, in the yard before letting them out.
    • Same "Potty" instructions

docs.title: What do you need/want?
docs.text:
• Parker understands this phrase and will try to direct/lead you physically or with his gaze. This can be used in general to figure out what he needs, e.g. a certain toy he's asking for or wanting to go outside.
  • Giles may direct you with his gaze in response to your question.

docs.title: Do you want water? / Drink your water
Do you want to go potty/outside? Let's go outside
docs.text: • They know you are urging them to go outside to go potty

docs.title: Where's your toy?
docs.text: • This is for Parker. He will bring a toy to you to play by/with you.

docs.title: Basic commands
docs.text:
• Sit
  • Down
  • Handshake
  • Other hand
    • Used immediately after handshake. The dogs will give you the other hand for a handshake
  • Stay
    • OK; this cancels "stay"
  • Come (here)
